root_text.an4.11:1.1: “Carato cepi, bhikkhave, bhikkhuno uppajjati kāmavitakko vā byāpādavitakko vā vihiṁsāvitakko vā.
root_text.an4.11:1.2: Tañce bhikkhu adhivāseti, nappajahati na vinodeti na byantīkaroti na anabhāvaṁ gameti,
root_text.an4.11:1.3: carampi, bhikkhave, bhikkhu evaṁbhūto ‘anātāpī anottāpī satataṁ samitaṁ kusīto hīnavīriyo’ti vuccati.
root_text.an4.11:2.1: Ṭhitassa cepi, bhikkhave, bhikkhuno uppajjati kāmavitakko vā byāpādavitakko vā vihiṁsāvitakko vā.
root_text.an4.11:2.2: Tañce bhikkhu adhivāseti, nappajahati na vinodeti na byantīkaroti na anabhāvaṁ gameti,
root_text.an4.11:2.3: ṭhitopi, bhikkhave, bhikkhu evaṁbhūto ‘anātāpī anottāpī satataṁ samitaṁ kusīto hīnavīriyo’ti vuccati.
root_text.an4.11:3.1: Nisinnassa cepi, bhikkhave, bhikkhuno uppajjati kāmavitakko vā byāpādavitakko vā vihiṁsāvitakko vā.
root_text.an4.11:3.2: Tañce bhikkhu adhivāseti, nappajahati na vinodeti na byantīkaroti na anabhāvaṁ gameti,
root_text.an4.11:3.3: nisinnopi, bhikkhave, bhikkhu evaṁbhūto ‘anātāpī anottāpī satataṁ samitaṁ kusīto hīnavīriyo’ti vuccati.
root_text.an4.11:4.1: Sayānassa cepi, bhikkhave, bhikkhuno jāgarassa uppajjati kāmavitakko vā byāpādavitakko vā vihiṁsāvitakko vā.
root_text.an4.11:4.2: Tañce bhikkhu adhivāseti, nappajahati na vinodeti na byantīkaroti na anabhāvaṁ gameti,
root_text.an4.11:4.3: sayānopi, bhikkhave, bhikkhu jāgaro evaṁbhūto ‘anātāpī anottāpī satataṁ samitaṁ kusīto hīnavīriyo’ti vuccati.
root_text.an4.11:5.1: Carato cepi, bhikkhave, bhikkhuno uppajjati kāmavitakko vā byāpādavitakko vā vihiṁsāvitakko vā.
root_text.an4.11:5.2: Tañce bhikkhu nādhivāseti, pajahati vinodeti byantīkaroti anabhāvaṁ gameti;
root_text.an4.11:5.3: carampi, bhikkhave, bhikkhu evaṁbhūto ‘ātāpī ottāpī satataṁ samitaṁ āraddhavīriyo pahitatto’ti vuccati.
root_text.an4.11:6.1: Ṭhitassa cepi, bhikkhave, bhikkhuno uppajjati kāmavitakko vā byāpādavitakko vā vihiṁsāvitakko vā.
root_text.an4.11:6.2: Tañce bhikkhu nādhivāseti, pajahati vinodeti byantīkaroti anabhāvaṁ gameti;
root_text.an4.11:6.3: ṭhitopi, bhikkhave, bhikkhu evaṁbhūto ‘ātāpī ottāpī satataṁ samitaṁ āraddhavīriyo pahitatto’ti vuccati.
root_text.an4.11:7.1: Nisinnassa cepi, bhikkhave, bhikkhuno uppajjati kāmavitakko vā byāpādavitakko vā vihiṁsāvitakko vā.
root_text.an4.11:7.2: Tañce bhikkhu nādhivāseti, pajahati vinodeti byantīkaroti anabhāvaṁ gameti;
root_text.an4.11:7.3: nisinnopi, bhikkhave, bhikkhu evaṁbhūto ‘ātāpī ottāpī satataṁ samitaṁ āraddhavīriyo pahitatto’ti vuccati.
root_text.an4.11:8.1: Sayānassa cepi, bhikkhave, bhikkhuno jāgarassa uppajjati kāmavitakko vā byāpādavitakko vā vihiṁsāvitakko vā.
root_text.an4.11:8.2: Tañce bhikkhu nādhivāseti, pajahati vinodeti byantīkaroti anabhāvaṁ gameti;
root_text.an4.11:8.3: sayānopi, bhikkhave, bhikkhu jāgaro evaṁbhūto ‘ātāpī ottāpī satataṁ samitaṁ āraddhavīriyo pahitatto’ti vuccatīti.
root_text.an4.11:11.1: Yo ca caraṁ vā tiṭṭhaṁ vā,

translation_text.an4.11:1.1: “Mendicants, suppose a mendicant has a sensual, malicious, or cruel thought while walking.
translation_text.an4.11:1.2: They tolerate it and don’t give it up, get rid of it, eliminate it, and obliterate it.
translation_text.an4.11:1.3: Such a mendicant is said to be ‘not keen or prudent, always lazy, and lacking energy’ when walking.
translation_text.an4.11:2.1: Suppose a mendicant has a sensual, malicious, or cruel thought while standing …
translation_text.an4.11:4.1: or when lying down while awake.
translation_text.an4.11:4.2: They tolerate it and don’t give it up, get rid of it, eliminate it, and obliterate it.
translation_text.an4.11:4.3: Such a mendicant is said to be ‘not keen or prudent, always lazy, and lacking energy’ when lying down while awake.
translation_text.an4.11:5.1: Suppose a mendicant has a sensual, malicious, or cruel thought while walking.
translation_text.an4.11:5.2: They don’t tolerate it, but give it up, get rid of it, eliminate it, and obliterate it.
translation_text.an4.11:5.3: Such a mendicant is said to be ‘keen and prudent, always energetic and resolute’ when walking.
translation_text.an4.11:6.1: Suppose a mendicant has a sensual, malicious, or cruel thought while standing …
translation_text.an4.11:8.1: or when lying down while awake.
translation_text.an4.11:8.2: They don’t tolerate it, but give it up, get rid of it, eliminate it, and obliterate it.
translation_text.an4.11:8.3: Such a mendicant is said to be ‘keen and prudent, always energetic and resolute’ when lying down while awake.”
translation_text.an4.11:9.3: if you think a bad thought
translation_text.an4.11:9.4: to do with the domestic life,
translation_text.an4.11:11.1: But one who, whether standing or walking,
